w:r.w:t: LETTER OF INTENT (“LOI”) - PURCHASE AND SALE OF LAND
This LOI covers the purchase and sale of land described below and in
BKV Temple energy campus, Temple, Texas, together with all improvements then located thereon, as further described in
, but excluding the acreage reserved by Seller for its land infrastructure (the “Excluded Land”), estimated at
~787 gross acres (~727 acres net of the ~
The portion of the acreage (other than the Excluded Land) that is developable for data center use, as mutually agreed by the parties or as determined by a mutually agreed site/civil study by
Modular - up to 400MW near term gas generation
Temple I/II (~750 MW) and Temple III (~600 MW)
~1,350 MW delivered behind-the-meter via a private use network (the “PUN”)
Power is supplied under the PPAs described in Exhibit C.
Interconnection agreements, including LLIS ERCOT confirmation
Power will be supplied behind-the-meter under three separate power purchase agreements – Modular (~400 MW), Temple I/II (~750 MW), and Temple III (~600 MW) Indicative pricing is set out in
. All PPA terms are indicative and remain subject to negotiation.

Energization of the PUN, including load approval (the “Power CPs”). Acreage powered by the modular generation satisfies its Power CPs at PSA Close (Day 1); all other Buildable Acreage satisfies its Power CPs upon PUN energization (Day N). Satisfaction of the Power CPs triggers the applicable $400,000/acre top-up under the Payment Schedule.

Energization is defined as when the specific power in a given tranche is constructed, energized, approved by all governing and regulating parties, and delivering power at the on-site project substation.

Approval, registration, or authorization of the PUN by ERCOT and the PUCT, in form and substance reasonably acceptable to Purchaser, permitting behind-the-meter delivery of the Committed Power to the Property.
120 days from the execution of this LOI
160 Days from the effective date of the Purchase Agreement
The Payments will be made per the “Payment Schedule”
This LOI, and all negotiations between the parties are subject to the nondisclosure agreement signed between Purchaser and Seller, dated May 7, 2026.
Purchaser and Seller will negotiate in good faith toward a purchase agreement and related documents (“
"). This LOI reflects the parties’ understanding of the terms for that Purchase Agreement.
The transaction structure contemplated by this LOI will be (i) the purchase and sale of the Property and (ii) the execution of the power purchase agreements described in Exhibit C (collectively, the
). The conclusion of the Transaction will be subject to the fulfillment of all Closing Conditions outlined below.

During the Exclusivity Period, Purchaser and Seller will negotiate exclusively and diligently in good faith for the Transaction with the intent to reach a mutually satisfactory Purchase Agreement. Neither Seller nor its representatives, directors, officers, or agents will, directly or indirectly, solicit, pursue, initiate, encourage, discuss or participate in any discussions or negotiations with any third party regarding the Transaction, the sale, lease, or other transaction regarding the Property or Interconnection Agreement. Seller will not allow any third party other than Purchaser or Purchaser’s representatives to inspect, test or obtain a survey or valuation of all or any portion of the Property. At the end of the Exclusivity Period, it will automatically extend for an additional sixty (60) days if the parties are negotiating in good faith, unless a party gives the other fourteen (14) days’ prior written notice of its intent to terminate prior to expiration of the initial Exclusivity Period. Seller will promptly notify Purchaser of any unsolicited proposal or inquiry regarding the sale, lease, or other transaction regarding the Property or Interconnection Agreement during the Exclusivity Period, including the revival of discussions that originated prior to commencement of the Exclusivity Period.

Purchaser will, within thirty (30) days from the later of (i) Execution Date of this LOI, or (ii) the date that Seller has obtained an ERCOT Batch Zero confirmation from the Utility confirming the utility Power Load Ramp is being studied, prepare the initial draft of the Purchase Agreement, including an assignment of the contract for the Committed Utility Power, on a commercially reasonable form. The “
” of this LOI shall be the date that the last party executes this LOI.

The Purchase Price is priced per acre: (a) at PSA Close (Day 1), Cost + 15% per acre for all acreage other than the Excluded Land; and (b) a top-up to $400,000 per acre for all Buildable Acreage, paid upon satisfaction of the Power CPs for such acreage (Day N). Acreage powered by the modular generation receives its top-up at PSA Close.

If the Buildable Acreage, the cost basis, or the power available at the Property differs from the assumptions in Exhibit B, the Purchase Price will be adjusted accordingly, as further outlined in the Purchase Agreement.

The Purchase Price is paid in two stages, with indicative aggregate amounts set out in the Payment Schedule (Exhibit B): (i) Day 1 (PSA Close): Cost + 15% per acre for all acreage other than the Excluded Land, plus the $400,000/acre top-up for the modular-powered data-center acreage; and (ii) Day N: the $400,000/acre top-up for all remaining Buildable Acreage, payable upon satisfaction of the Power CPs (PUN energization, including load approval). The parties will discuss security or other protection for Purchaser if the Power CPs are delayed.
CONDITIONS TO THE DAY N PAYMENT:

The Day N $400,000/acre top-up is payable, with respect to each tranche of Buildable Acreage, only upon satisfaction for that acreage of: (i) the Power CPs; (ii) PUN Regulatory Approval; (iii) written confirmation from the Utility that the Interconnection Agreement may be assigned to Purchaser or its designee for datacenter use for the full Committed Power, and assignment of the Interconnection Agreement to Purchaser; (iv) execution of a binding ESA or equivalent agreement with the Utility confirming delivery of the full Committed Power, with a Power Load Ramp consistent with this LOI, firm energization dates to the on-site substation, and security collateral and minimum capacity payments acceptable to Purchaser; and (v) Purchaser and Seller having obtained all governmental and regulatory permits, agreements, approvals, consents, and entitlements necessary for a data center supporting the Committed Power on the Property (the “Permits”), which may include zoning, site plan, and building permits, environmental clearances, utility connections, and any special or conditional use permits. These conditions are separate from, and in addition to, the Closing Conditions to the Day 1 purchase; the Day 1 purchase and Cost + 15% payment are not conditioned on PUN energization, PUN Regulatory Approval, the ESA, the Interconnection Agreement, or the Permits.

Seller will diligently pursue PUN Regulatory Approval and keep Purchaser reasonably informed of its status. If PUN Regulatory Approval is not obtained on or before the PUN Approval Longstop Date, Purchaser may, in its sole discretion, terminate the Purchase Agreement, reconvey the Property to Seller, and require Seller to refund the Day 1 portion of the Purchase Price paid by Purchaser, together with interest at [__]% per annum and Purchaser’s documented carrying costs, within thirty (30) days of such election. To secure Seller’s refund obligation, Seller will, at Purchaser’s election, either (i) hold the Day 1 portion of the Purchase Price in escrow pending PUN Regulatory Approval, or (ii) deliver a letter of credit or creditworthy parent guaranty in the amount of the Day 1 portion of the Purchase Price.

Purchaser will have the Diligence Period to investigate the Property. Within 5 days after the Execution Date, Seller will deliver to Purchaser all records in Seller's possession relating to the Property, including environmental reports, geotechnical studies, zoning and engineering reports, surveys, title reports, tax statements, and information on existing easements or rights-of-way that may need to be terminated for Purchaser's development ("

"). Purchaser's due diligence will include (and Seller will support) confirming a viable path to clean, marketable, and insurable title sufficient for datacenter development, including the ability to cure or terminate any Identified Conditions. Seller will provide Purchaser and its representatives reasonable access to the Property for inspections, testing, and surveys. The Purchase Agreement will automatically terminate at the end of the Diligence Period unless Purchaser delivers written notice to Seller confirming its election to proceed. If the Purchase Agreement terminates under this provision, the Escrow Agent must return the Deposit to Purchaser within 5 business days.

Within 10 days after full execution of the Purchase Agreement, Seller will obtain, at Seller's expense, a title commitment from a national title company selected by Purchaser. Seller will deliver any existing ALTA survey in its possession; Purchaser may obtain a new or updated ALTA survey at its own expense. Seller must convey fee simple, marketable, and insurable title by warranty deed, free of all liens and encumbrances except those approved by Purchaser ("

"). Purchaser may object to title or survey matters through the end of the Diligence Period. If Seller does not cure objected matters, Purchaser may terminate the Purchase Agreement. Seller must satisfy all monetary liens at or before Closing. Issuance of an owner's title policy (subject only to Permitted Encumbrances) is at Seller's expense and is a condition to Closing.
Closing will be 60 days following execution of the Purchase Agreement, in each case subject to the fulfillment of all Closing Conditions.
In addition to other customary conditions to close, Purchaser’s obligation to purchase the Property will include the following closing conditions (collectively “
Seller will obtain fee-simple, marketable, and insurable title to the Property before the Closing Date.

Seller will grant to Purchaser all easements and approvals (and all other applicable rights and documents, if any) under Seller’s direct or indirect control, as required for Purchaser’s development of the Property.
The Property will be free from person, goods, or encumbrances of any kind, including third party rights.
The Property will be free and clear of any environmental contamination or hazardous materials.
All representations and warranties of Seller in the Purchase Agreement will remain true and accurate.

Seller agrees to abandon or terminate, or cause its owners, directors of affiliates to abandon or terminate, at Closing, all Identified Conditions for which Seller, its owners, directors or affiliates can abandon or terminate. Seller will assist and cooperate with Purchaser at Purchaser’s request in obtaining any third-party easements, approvals and crossing agreements, as required for Purchaser’s development of the Property.

If the Closing Conditions are not met by the Closing Date, Purchaser can: (i) terminate the Purchase Agreement; (ii) extend Closing up to thirty (30) days after the scheduled Closing Date, or (iii) waive any Closing Conditions and proceed to Closing. If the Closing Conditions are not met within thirty (30) days after the scheduled Closing Date, Purchaser can elect to: (i) terminate the Purchase Agreement, (ii) waive any unmet Closing Conditions and proceed to Closing. The foregoing remedies will not be exclusive but will be cumulative and in addition to all other remedies available.

The Purchase Price will be the total aggregate amount that will be paid to Seller under this LOI and Purchase Agreement. Any additional sums required in connection with the same, including but not limited to transmission upgrade payments, letters of credit to the utility, or land option payments will be paid by Seller, or if paid by Purchaser, will be deducted from the Purchase Price.

After execution of this LOI, Seller will not grant, record, join into, or consent to, any easements, rights-of-way, other encumbrances, or agreements affecting or over or through the Property or enter into any agreements, proffers, or other commitments which would affect the Property following Closing without obtaining the prior written consent of Purchaser, which consent may be withheld by Purchaser in its sole and absolute discretion.

Each party will bear all of its own expenses (including the expenses of its attorneys, advisors, accountants, consultants or other experts or representatives retained by them) incurred in connection with the negotiation and execution of this LOI, the negotiation and any consummation of the Purchase Agreement, any other fees described in the Purchase Agreement, and related matters.
The contents of this LOI and the Transaction will be governed by the NDA.

The LOI is not a binding or enforceable agreement between Seller or Purchaser, except for the good faith, Exclusivity, Expenses and Confidentiality provisions above. Each party can decline to enter into the Purchase Agreement without liability, except for breach of the Exclusivity, Expenses and Confidentiality provisions above.

This Letter will be governed by the laws of the state of New York (USA), without regard to conflicts of laws principles. The parties will refer any dispute or claim arising under this Letter for resolution by courts in New York, New York.
This LOI is only effective if signed by both parties on or before 5:00 pm Eastern Standard Time, ten (10) business days from the date of this LOI (“
”). If this LOI is not executed by both Seller and Purchaser by the Execution Deadline, this LOI terminates automatically.
Attach description of land and any applicable maps or images

All amounts below are indicative and subject to confirmation of Seller’s documented land cost, the final Excluded Land acreage, and the mutually agreed Buildable Acreage. Figures assume ~787 gross acres; ~
acres of Excluded Land (~727 net acres); a land cost basis of ~$
/acre (Cost + 15% ≈ $172,500/acre); and a value of $400,000/acre for Buildable Acreage.
Execution and closing of the Purchase Agreement
Cost + 15% per acre on ~727 net acres, plus the $400,000/acre top-up on the ~
$400,000/acre top-up on the remaining Buildable Acreage (~

Day N and Total scale with the final Buildable Acreage: at 80% buildable, Day N ≈ $132MM (Total ≈ $264MM); at 90% buildable, Day N ≈ $149MM (Total ≈ $281MM). All figures are rounded and exclude transaction costs.

Power is supplied behind-the-meter under three power purchase agreements, each structured as capacity payment + FOM + VOM + Fuel. The Modular and Temple I/II PPAs will be grouped (execution of the Modular PPA is conditioned on execution of the Temple I/II PPA). All pricing below is indicative and remains subject to negotiation.

Modular capacity of ~400 MW serves ~324 MW of utility load (basis of design). Each PPA is structured as capacity payment + FOM + VOM + Fuel; ranges shown both excluding and including fuel. All figures are indicative and subject to negotiation.
